sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis — To-Do List App
sections.content: **Scope:** `app.js` (243 lines) is the only application logic; `index.html`/`styles.css` are markup/style. There is **no test infrastructure at all** — no `package.json`, no test runner, no test files anywhere in the repo. Coverage is effectively **0%**.

sections.title: Structural blocker (read this before the skeletons)
sections.content:
`app.js` exports nothing — it's a plain script that queries the DOM and wires listeners at top-level, then immediately self-invokes (`loadTodos(); updateFilterButtons(); render();` at line 240-242). Two consequences:

1. **No function is importable**, so true unit tests need either (a) a small refactor adding `export` statements, or (b) black-box integration tests that load `index.html` into jsdom and `require('./app.js')` as a side-effecting script.
2. Several DOM refs (`todoForm`, `todoInput`, `todoList`, `activeText`) have **no null-guard** before `.addEventListener` (unlike `clearCompletedBtn`/`markAllBtn`, which do check). If the HTML markup ever drifts from these ids, the script throws at load time with no error handling — itself a gap worth a test.

Recommend **Vitest + jsdom** (zero-config, fast, works with plain scripts). I did not create files/configs since this was requested as analysis only — skeletons below assume that setup.

---

sections.title: 1. Untested functions/classes (all 19)
sections.content:
| Function | Risk if broken |
|---|---|
| `loadTodos`, `saveTodos` | data loss / silent reset |
| `createId` | duplicate ids → wrong item edited/deleted |
| `sync`, `render`, `createTodoItem` | UI desync |
| `getActiveCount`, `getCompletedCount` | wrong counter/disabled state |
| `addTodo`, `updateTodo`, `toggleTodo`, `deleteTodo` | core CRUD correctness |
| `clearCompleted`, `markAllCompleted` | bulk-action correctness |
| `setFilter`, `filteredTodos`, `updateFilterButtons` | filter UI correctness |
| `startEdit` | inline-edit correctness |
| `handleFormSubmit` | form UX |

sections.title: 2. Edge cases not covered
sections.content:
- `loadTodos()`: malformed JSON in localStorage, valid JSON that isn't an array (e.g. `{}`), array of malformed todo objects (missing `id`/`completed`/`text`).
- `addTodo("   ")`: whitespace-only input must be rejected.
- `updateTodo`/`toggleTodo`/`deleteTodo` called with a **non-existent id**: should no-op, not throw.
- `clearCompleted()`/`markAllCompleted()` on an **empty list**: must early-return and skip `sync()` (no spurious save/render).
- `filteredTodos()` with an **unrecognized filter value**: falls through to "all" — currently undocumented behavior.
- `startEdit`: Enter then blur firing in sequence (the `finished` guard should prevent double-save) — and Escape discarding edits, including when the edited value is empty.
- Cross-tab sync via the `storage` event: should ignore events for unrelated keys.

sections.title: 3. Missing error-handling tests
sections.content:
- `saveTodos()` has **no try/catch** — if `localStorage.setItem` throws (quota exceeded, private-mode Safari, storage disabled), the exception propagates uncaught and the in-memory state and persisted state diverge silently.
- Missing required DOM elements at script load (id typo in HTML) → unguarded `.addEventListener` throws; no fallback/error message.
- `loadTodos()`'s catch path only logs a warning — there's no test confirming the app still functions afterward (i.e. `state.todos` resets to `[]` and `render()` doesn't crash).

sections.title: 4. Integration test gaps
sections.content:
- End-to-end flow: type → submit → item appears → check → counts update → delete → empty state message.
- Filter switching reflecting both the active button style (`updateFilterButtons`) and the rendered list together.
- Persistence round-trip: add items, reload the "page" (re-run `loadTodos()`/`render()`), confirm state survives.
- Cross-tab sync: dispatch a `storage` event and confirm the UI re-renders from the new localStorage value.
- Bootstrap-time failure mode (missing DOM nodes) — currently untested entirely.

---

sections.title: Test skeletons

rawOutputPreview:
## Test Coverage Analysis — To-Do List App

**Scope:** `app.js` (243 lines) is the only application logic; `index.html`/`styles.css` are markup/style. There is **no test infrastructure at all** — no `package.json`, no test runner, no test files anywhere in the repo. Coverage is effectively **0%**.

### Structural blocker (read this before the skeletons)

`app.js` exports nothing — it's a plain script that queries the DOM and wires listeners at top-level, then immediately self-invokes (`loadTodos(); updateFilterButtons(); render();` at line 240-242). Two consequences:

1. **No function is importable**, so true unit tests need either (a) a small refactor adding `export` statements, or (b) black-box integration tests that load `index.html` into jsdom and `require('./app.js')` as a side-effecting script.
2. Several DOM refs (`todoForm`, `todoInput`, `todoList`, `activeText`) have **no null-guard** before `.addEventListener` (unlike `clearCompletedBtn`/`markAllBtn`, which do check). If the HTML markup ever drifts from these ids, the script throws at load time with no error handling — itself a gap worth a test.

Recommend **Vitest + jsdom** (zero-config, fast, works with plain scripts). I did not create files/configs since this was requested as analysis only — skeletons below assume that setup.

---

### 1. Untested functions/classes (all 19)

| Function | Risk if broken |
|---|---|
| `loadTodos`, `saveTodos` | data loss / silent reset |
| `createId` | duplicate ids → wrong item edited/deleted |
| `sync`, `render`, `createTodoItem` | UI desync |
| `getActiveCount`, `getCompletedCount` | wrong counter/disabled state |
| `addTodo`, `updateTodo`, `toggleTodo`, `deleteTodo` | core CRUD correctness |
| `clearCompleted`, `markAllCompleted` | bulk-action correctness |
| `setFilter`, `filteredTodos`, `updateFilterButtons` | filter UI correctness |
| `startEdit` | inline-edit correctness |
| `handleFormSubmit` | form UX |

### 2. Edge cases not covered

- `loadTodos()`: malformed
